message:
fix(runtime): session channel send fallibility, IO flush-before-shutdown, NATS sync close, timer wheel modulo

Four independent correctness fixes across runtime subsystems:

1. TrackedPermit::send() now returns Result<CommittedProof, SendError>
   instead of infallibly consuming the permit. If the receiver was dropped
   between reserve() and send(), the obligation is properly aborted rather
   than committed for a delivery that never happened. Tests updated to
   unwrap the new Result.

2. Bidirectional IO copy (copy.rs) now flushes the write side before
   initiating shutdown, in both A→B and B→A transfer directions. Without
   this, buffered data could be lost when the write side has pending bytes
   at the time poll_shutdown is called.

3. HTTP/1.1 server (server.rs) now gracefully shuts down the connection
   via AsyncWriteExt::shutdown() after the request loop completes, ensuring
   TLS close_notify is sent and the peer observes clean connection closure.

4. NatsClient::close() converted from async to sync (the body was already
   synchronous, evidenced by the clippy::unused_async allow). Now also
   calls stream.shutdown(Both) to close the TCP socket immediately rather
   than relying on drop ordering.

5. Timer wheel modulo fix (intrusive_wheel.rs + wheel.rs): slot index
   computation now applies modulo before casting to usize, preventing
   truncation on 32-bit targets where large tick values would wrap
   incorrectly. Changed from `(tick as usize) % SLOTS` to
   `(tick % (SLOTS as u128/u64)) as usize`.

6. src/types/cancel.rs: rustfmt on severity test assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
